paper_title: Evaluation of Staminate Flower and Pollen Production in Watermelon Cultigens

methods.description: This method describes the agricultural practices used to prepare the field and plant watermelon seeds for the experiment. It includes soil preparation, fertilization, fumigation, and planting procedures.

steps.description: Nitrogen and potash (K2O) were broadcast and disk incorporated (224 kg·ha-1 of 15N-0P-40K) before fumigation with 1,3-dicholoropropene and chloropicrin (Telone C-17 at 93 L·ha-1).
steps.searchable_summary: apply nitrogen and potash fertilizers and perform soil fumigation

steps.description: Rows within plots were covered with black plastic (1.25 mm) and a single drip-irrigation tube was placed just beneath the soil surface in the center of each bed.
steps.searchable_summary: install black plastic mulch and drip irrigation system

steps.description: Seeds were planted in the greenhouse in a soilless growing medium in 4 × 4-cm transplant trays for 4 weeks and then field transplanted in early May of each year.
steps.searchable_summary: plant seeds in greenhouse and transplant to field

steps.description: Transplants were drenched in a starter fertilizer solution containing insecticide for soil insect control.
steps.searchable_summary: apply starter fertilizer and insecticide to transplants

methods.description: This method involves collecting data on staminate flower production during the peak flowering phase to evaluate the reproductive potential of watermelon cultigens.

steps.description: Open staminate flowers were quantified between 0800 and 1100 HR in each of four plots on four separate days in both years at 4-d intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: count open staminate flowers in plots

steps.description: These four values were averaged and divided by 40 to obtain the mean number of staminate flowers produced per plant per day for each cultigen.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean staminate flowers per plant per day
outputs: mean flowers per plant per day

methods.description: This method describes the collection and processing of pollen from staminate flowers to determine total pollen production.

steps.description: Staminate flowers were collected 10 to 30 min before anthesis and placed in screened containers to prevent pollen loss.
Once corolla expansion and anther dehiscence were confirmed, the corolla was removed and each flower base with anthers was placed into vials containing a preservation solution.
Vials were shaken vigorously to dislodge pollen grains, and the flower base was removed and washed again to ensure pollen removal.

methods.description: This method quantifies the number of pollen grains per flower using a particle counter and confirms accuracy with a hemacytometer.

steps.description: Stored pollen samples were filtered to remove extraneous plant material and diluted with saline to a final volume.
Estimates of pollen levels were obtained by taking subsamples and multiplying by the dilution factor to derive the estimated total number of pollen grains per sample.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate total pollen grains per sample

steps.description: The accuracy and precision of particle counter readings were confirmed using a hemacytometer.
steps.searchable_summary: validate pollen count accuracy with hemacytometer

methods.description: This method involves statistical analysis of the experimental data to compare staminate flower, pollen production, and male reproductive output.

steps.description: Staminate flower, pollen production, and male reproductive output were compared using analysis of variance (PROC GLM) and mean separations (PROC MEANS) with SAS version 6.12.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOVA and mean separation analysis

steps.description: Pearson correlation analyses were performed to detect possible relationships between early fruit set and flower production.
